Off Calendar Apr-16-2020 Continued To Jun-16-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion For Assignment Order
Off Calendar Feb-13-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
E. Jeffrey Banchero, Esq., 415-955-9101, appearing for 547-551 Hayes LLC, Plaintiff and moving party.

Janet Brayer, Esq., 415-445-9555, appearing for Public Bikes, for Defendant and opposing party.

Argued. Mr. Banchero stated that he contested to the tentative ruling, that the money needed for the accounting should come from the frozen assets and argued why the funds should be placed with the court. Ms. Brayer reported that she withdraws her request for accounting and stated that information can be obtained through discovery. The Court passes the matter to allow parties to dicuss the proposed order. The matter is recalled and Mr. Banchero stated that he will prepare a clean order, email Ms. Brayer confirming that she approves as to form and will return on February 14, 2020 to submit for review.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling with modifications appoved by all parties as follows:

PLAINTIFF 547-551 HAYES LLC. A LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY Motion For Assignment Order is GRANTED as requested. Defendant may seek an order terminating this assignment if necessary and proper upon the resolution of Defendant's Petition for Judicial Supervision of Voluntary Winding up and Dissolution of Corporation. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC3.1312(b). Judge Samuel K. Feng, Clerk: N. Wong, Not reported (D501)

Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order And Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment At 115 To Public Bikes
Off Calendar Oct-24-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Janet Brayer, 415.445.9555 for Plaintiff/moving party 547.551 Hayes LLC.
E. Jeffrey Banchero, 415.955.9101 for Defendants/opposing parties Public Bikes and Rob Forbes.

Hearing is held. Argued re: notice of acknowledgment and jurisdiction; proper service, sufficient capital, under capitalized, sold assests, liabilities, payment to creditors, alter ego, and unity of interests. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF 547-551 HAYES LLC. A LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order And Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment At 115 To Public Bikes is DENIED without prejudice. No proper service. Order submitted Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: A. Pascual, Not Reported (D501)
